concept.themeDescription: Outline the bright lights and dark shadows of city life after sunset.

technique.approach: Layered washes to depict contrasting values

executionSteps.description: Begin with a dry textured paper to enhance the fluidity and bleeding of colors.
Apply a light wash of phthalo green and ultramarine to set the base for the night sky.
While the first layer is still damp, introduce deeper shades like indigo and prussian blue to suggest depth and the darkness of the night.
Drop in hints of black sparingly to represent the dark silhouettes of buildings and cityscape structures.
Use a fine brush to splash or gently stipple cadmium yellow light and yellow ochre to represent the city lights, ensuring a random but balanced distribution.
Enhance brightness in areas representing light by adding luminous whites, possibly mixing with a bit of water to create a glow effect around the lights.

Allow layers to dry intermittently. Use a spray bottle or a brush with clean water to soften and merge some of the sections to create a more cohesive yet blurry effect typical of lights at night seen from a distance.
Re-evaluate the painting for balance between light and dark, adding touches where needed to increase contrast or diffuse too harsh edges.
Finish by reviewing the composition from a distance to ensure the abstract portrayal resonates with the emotional and visual impact of a cityscape at night.

concept.finalThoughts: The objective of this abstract watercolor is to evoke the vibrant yet mysterious ambiance of cityscapes at night, focusing on the interplay between light and dark, rather than defined outlines.
